findings.description: New smtplib.SMTP connection opened per message inside the flush() loop (TCP+EHLO+QUIT per send, ~50-300ms overhead each; 1000 messages = 1000 connections plus relay rate-limit risk) instead of one reused connection hoisted outside the loop.
smtplib.SMTP created without a timeout, so a hung relay blocks flush() and the calling pipeline indefinitely; pass timeout= explicitly.

flush() clears _pending but never _seen, leaving an unbounded dedup cache (no max size/TTL) that grows for the process lifetime in a long-running pipeline; clear per-batch or bound with LRU/TTL (cross-flush blocking side effect deferred to critic-logic).
EmailQueue mutates _pending/_seen/stats with no synchronization, racy if the pipeline enqueues or flushes from multiple threads (speculative; usage unknown).
Unvalidated msg.to/msg.subject interpolated into raw CRLF-delimited headers in _render enables SMTP header injection - brief note, detail deferred to critic-security.
Mail sent over plaintext SMTP on port 587 with no STARTTLS or authentication - brief note, deferred to critic-security.
A sendmail exception mid-loop propagates before _pending.clear() runs, leaving already-sent messages queued so a retry of flush() re-sends duplicates - brief note, deferred to critic-logic.
Dedup key is recipient address only, so a second distinct message (different subject/body) to the same recipient is silently dropped - brief note, deferred to critic-logic.
MIMEText is imported but never used; dead import.
